referencedTypes.name: PopoverPositionerProps
referencedTypes.resolvedType: { placement?: Placement; side?: Side; sideOffset?: number; sideFlip?: boolean; align?: Align; alignOffset?: number; alignFlip?: boolean; avoidCollisions?: boolean; collisionBoundary?: Element | (Element | null)[] | null; collisionPadding?: Padding; arrowPadding?: number; hideShiftedArrow?: boolean; sticky?: 'partial' | 'always'; hideWhenDetached?: boolean; positionStrategy?: 'fixed' | 'absolute'; updatePositionStrategy?: 'always' | 'optimized'; disableUpdateOnLayoutShift?: boolean; prioritizePosition?: boolean; reference?: ReferenceElement; forceMount?: boolean }

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.

members.name: side
members.description:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.

members.name: sideOffset
members.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

members.name: sideFlip
members.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

members.name: align
members.description: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.

members.name: alignOffset
members.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

members.name: alignFlip
members.description:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.

members.name: avoidCollisions
members.description: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

members.name: collisionBoundary
members.description:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.

members.name: collisionPadding
members.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

members.name: arrowPadding
members.description:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.

members.name: hideShiftedArrow
members.description:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.

members.name: sticky
members.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

members.name: hideWhenDetached
members.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

members.name: positionStrategy
members.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

members.name: updatePositionStrategy
members.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.name: disableUpdateOnLayoutShift
members.description: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

members.name: prioritizePosition
members.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

members.name: reference
members.description:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

members.name: to
members.description:
Vue native teleport component prop `:to`

{@link https://vuejs.org/guide/built-ins/teleport.html#basic-usage}

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Disable teleport and render the component inline

{@link https://vuejs.org/guide/built-ins/teleport.html#disabling-teleport}

members.name: defer
members.description:
Defer the resolving of a Teleport target until other parts of the application have mounted (requires Vue 3.5.0+)

{@link https://vuejs.org/guide/built-ins/teleport.html#deferred-teleport}

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

members.name: open
members.description: The controlled open state of the dialog. Can be bound with `v-model:open`.

members.name: defaultOpen
members.description: The open state of the dialog when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.

members.name: modal
members.description:
The modality of the dialog. When set to `true`, interaction with outside elements will be disabled and only dialog
content will be visible to screen readers.

emits.name: PopconfirmEmits
emits.resolvedType: { "update:open": [value: boolean]; escapeKeyDown: [event: KeyboardEvent]; pointerDownOutside: [event: PointerDownOutsideEvent]; focusOutside: [event: FocusOutsideEvent]; interactOutside: [event: PointerDownOutsideEvent | FocusOutsideEvent]; openAutoFocus: [event: Event]; closeAutoFocus: [event: Event]; close: [] }

callables.name: update:open
callables.description: Event handler called when the open state of the dialog changes.

callables.name: escapeKeyDown
callables.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: KeyboardEvent
referencedTypes.description:
**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. The event type (keydown, keypress, or keyup) identifies what kind of keyboard activity occurred.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)

callables.name: pointerDownOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: focusOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: interactOutside
callables.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.

callables.name: openAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: Event
referencedTypes.description:
The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)

callables.name: closeAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on close. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: Event
referencedTypes.description:
The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)
